User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Vexa AI platform.

signupDate.description: The date and time the user signed up.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a single message in a chat conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat message.

userId.description: Reference to the User who sent the message. (Relationship: User 1:N ChatMessage)

timestamp.description: The date and time the message was sent.

content.description: The text content of the message.

isUserGenerated.description: Indicates whether the message was generated by the user or the AI chatbot.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership: Only the user with the matching {userId} can access their profile data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores chat messages for each user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the chat messages.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat message.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to store user data and chat messages for the Vexa AI application. It leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data and ensures authorization independence through denormalization, especially for chat messages. The structure supports the QAPs by segregating user data and chat history into dedicated collections with clear ownership rules, enabling secure list operations.

The `users/{userId}` collection stores user profiles. The `users/{userId}/chat_messages/{messageId}` collection stores chat messages associated with each user, where `userId` is denormalized into each `ChatMessage` document. This denormalization is crucial for authorization independence; security rules can validate that the authenticated user matches the `userId` stored within the message itself, without needing to perform costly `get()` operations to verify ownership up the hierarchy. This approach also facilitates secure list operations, as rules can efficiently filter messages based on the denormalized `userId` without relying on complex queries.
